county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Madison County formed 1846 from Unorganized territory. County seat is Winterset.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Winterset holds deeds from 1849; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Madison County is famous for its covered bridges (six historic covered bridges survive). Winterset is the birthplace of John Wayne. Madison County Historical Society in Winterset. Settlers came from Ohio, Indiana, and Kentucky. Middle River and North River valleys. Pammel State Park area. St. Charles and Earlham communities. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Madison County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Madison County Deed Records -- County Recorder
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Winterset
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1846 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: Madison County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Winterset
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1846. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Madison County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Winterset / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1846. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Madison County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Madison County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Madison County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1850-1950 for Madison County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Madison County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Madison County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Madison County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Madison County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Madison County Auditor, Winterset
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Madison County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Mahaska County formed 1843 from Indian Cession (Keokuk Reserve). County seat is Oskaloosa.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Oskaloosa holds deeds from 1844; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Mahaska County was named for Chief Mahaska (White Cloud) of the Ioway people. Oskaloosa was an important Quaker center -- Oskaloosa Friends Meeting records. William Penn College (now William Penn University, est. 1873) is a Quaker institution. Coal mining community in the Beacon and Muchakinock areas -- Muchakinock was a significant early African American mining community. Mahaska County Historical Society. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Mahaska County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Oskaloosa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1843. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Oskaloosa / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1843. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Mahaska County Quaker (Friends) Meeting Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Yearly Meeting / Swarthmore College Friends Historical Library / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Friends meeting records including minutes, membership lists, certificates of removal, birth/death/marriage registers. Mahaska County Quaker meetings were active in the Underground Railroad.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Marion County formed 1845 from Washington. County seat is Knoxville.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Knoxville holds deeds from 1845; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Marion County was an early settlement area with Red Rock Lake (now reservoir) and the Des Moines River. Knoxville Raceway (sprint car racing heritage). Marion County Historical Society. Pella, founded by Dutch immigrants under Rev. Hendrik Pieter Scholte in 1847, is one of Iowa's most significant Dutch settlements. Pella Historical Society and Scholte House archives. Dutch Reformed church records essential. Central University of Iowa (Pella, closed 1922) records. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Marion County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1845. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1845. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Marshall County formed 1846 from Jasper. County seat is Marshalltown.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Marshalltown holds deeds from 1849; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Marshall County is centered on Marshalltown, an industrial and railroad city. Fisher Controls (Emerson Electric) employment records. Strong Marshalltown community -- diverse immigrant population including Irish, German, and later Latino communities in meatpacking. Marshall County Historical Society. Meskwaki (Fox) Settlement is partially in Marshall County (see also Tama County). State Center and Albion communities. Iowa River valley. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Marshall County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Mills County formed 1851 from Pottawattamie. County seat is Glenwood.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Glenwood holds deeds from 1851; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Mills County on the Missouri River was part of the Mormon Trail corridor -- temporary Mormon settlements existed here in 1846-1847. Glenwood State Resource Center (formerly State Institution, est. 1876) institutional records at State Archives. Mills County Historical Society. Silver City, Malvern, and Pacific Junction communities. West Nishnabotna River. Loess Hills. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Mills County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Mills County Auditor, Glenwood
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Mills County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Mormon Trail Records -- Mills County Area
source_profiles.repository: LDS Church History Library, Salt Lake City / State Historical Society of Iowa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Latter-day Saint emigrant records, pioneer journals, and camp records for the Mills County area of the Mormon Trail across Iowa (1846-1869). Includes both LDS members and non-Mormon settlers who arrived along the same corridor.

source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Glenwood State Resource Center (est. 1876)
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Institutional records including admission registers, patient/inmate records, and related administrative files. Access may be restricted for recent records due to privacy laws.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Mitchell County formed 1851 from Chickasaw. County seat is Osage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Osage holds deeds from 1854; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Mitchell County was settled by New Englanders and Norwegians. Hamlin Garland, Pulitzer Prize author, grew up in Osage. Mitchell County Historical Society. Cedar River headwaters. St. Ansgar -- strong Norwegian heritage community with Lutheran church records. Stacyville and Riceville communities. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Mitchell County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Norwegian and Swedish Lutheran church records for Mitchell County congregations. Baptisms, confirmations, marriages, burials. Many Norwegian Synod and Hauge Synod congregations.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Monona County formed 1851 from Harrison. County seat is Onawa.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Onawa holds deeds from 1854; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Monona County on the Missouri River includes the Loess Hills. Lewis and Clark expedition passed through this area in 1804. Onawa is near the site where the Lewis and Clark party held council with local tribes. Turin Man archaeological site. Monona County Historical Society. Mapleton and Whiting communities. Soldier River and Little Sioux River valleys. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Monona County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Monroe County formed 1843 from Wapello. County seat is Albia.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Albia holds deeds from 1845; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Monroe County was a major coal mining region in south-central Iowa. Buxton, a company coal town (Consolidation Coal Company), was a remarkable interracial community (1895-1927) where African American miners lived alongside Swedish, Welsh, and other immigrant workers. Buxton records at Iowa State Archives and State Historical Society are nationally significant. Monroe County Historical Society. Albia's town square. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Monroe County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Montgomery County formed 1851 from Cass. County seat is Red Oak.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Red Oak holds deeds from 1856; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Montgomery County was settled in the 1860s-1870s. Red Oak was a significant railroad town. Montgomery County Historical Society. Villisca, site of the 1912 Villisca axe murders, has related court and newspaper records. Stanton's Swedish heritage (coffee cup and coffee pot water towers). East Nishnabotna River valley. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Montgomery County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Montgomery County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1860-1950 for Montgomery County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Montgomery County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Montgomery County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Montgomery County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Montgomery County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Montgomery County Auditor, Red Oak
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Montgomery County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Muscatine County formed 1836 from Des Moines. County seat is Muscatine.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Muscatine holds deeds from 1837; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Muscatine County is one of the earliest Iowa counties. The city of Muscatine was the 'Pearl Button Capital of the World' -- pearl button factory records document German, African American, and other worker communities. Muscatine Art Center and History Center. Mark Twain briefly lived in Muscatine. Muscatine County Genealogical Society. Wilton and West Liberty communities (West Liberty has a significant Latino community). FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Muscatine County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Muscatine County Deed Records -- County Recorder
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Muscatine
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1836 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: Muscatine County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Muscatine
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1836. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Muscatine County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Muscatine / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1836. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Muscatine County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Muscatine County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Muscatine County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1840-1950 for Muscatine County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Muscatine County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Muscatine County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Muscatine County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Muscatine County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Muscatine County Auditor, Muscatine
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Muscatine County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Muscatine County Early Church Records -- Mississippi River Settlements
source_profiles.repository: Various -- diocesan archives, denominational archives, FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Church registers (baptisms, marriages, burials) from early Mississippi River settlements in Muscatine County. Catholic, Lutheran, Methodist, and Presbyterian records. German and Irish congregations predominate.

source_profiles.source_name: Muscatine County Historic Newspapers
source_profiles.repository: State Historical Society of Iowa / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Muscatine County communities. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage announcements, land sale notices. State Historical Society of Iowa holds microfilm of many Iowa newspapers.
